entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop characterized by significant size and diversity in its genetic collections.

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop characterized by significant size and diversity in its genetic collections.

entities.name: U.S.
entities.description: Location of a large sorghum collection containing 40,570 accessions.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: Location of a large pearl millet collection containing 21,191 accessions.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing crop characteristics using genetic resources.

entities.name: CURATION
entities.description: The process involving acquisition, maintenance, characterization, and utilization of genetic resources.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: The diversity of genetic material within crop collections.

entities.name: MARGINAL AGRICULTURAL LAND
entities.description: Low-productivity land where the cultivation of sorghum and pearl millet is increasing.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of crops to withstand non-living environmental stresses.

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of crops to withstand living environmental stresses.

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: The genetic resources available for exploitation in crop improvement.

entities.name: TRADITIONAL METHODS
entities.description: Conventional techniques used to understand genetic variation and enhance crops.

entities.name: BIOTECHNICAL METHODS
entities.description: Modern biological techniques used to understand genetic variation and enhance crops.

entities.name: CROP ENHANCEMENT
entities.description: The goal of using genetic variation to improve crop performance.

entities.name: INFORMATION SHARING
entities.description: The global exchange of ideas and information to facilitate crop research.
